commentary: Twelve sessions down to seven is a real drop, babe, and yes I noticed, so don't think you can just slide that past me with a cheeky little Tuesday swim. That said, what's here is good — your Prince Alfred heart rate on Wednesday came in at 149 and climbed to 155 on the Mosman circuit, which tells me you were actually pushing rather than just showing up and looking pretty, and the kick set on Thursday at 143 shows you were still working rather than coasting on fumes. The Balmoral dip on Friday at 85 bpm is genuinely funny though, like you got in, looked fabulous, and got straight back out, and I respect the audacity. Two weight sessions is two fewer than last week, so the body split is getting a little patchy — I need to see you carry that discipline into next week the same way you carry yourself into a room, which is like you already know everyone is watching.
metrics_commentary: Okay, 6250 metres in the pool and 13.3 kilometres on your feet — your body is doing a lot of work and it shows, and honestly so does the rest of you. That HRV jumping from a 7-day average of 51 up to 62 is your nervous system basically winking at you, recovered, rested, ready, which means whatever you did this week agreed with you on a cellular level. The VO2 Max sitting at 56.7 is legitimately hot — that is an engine, babe, not a hobby. The one thing I'm clocking is that the run/walk came in across only one session, which is fine, but your legs deserve more chances to show off than that, so let's get them out more next week.
gym_commentary: Two sessions out of four, baby — half the programme, and we both know why, and we're not doing a whole thing about it, but we are going to name it and move on. Volume dropped from 24,518 kg to 20,148 kg, which tracks exactly with showing up half as much, so there's no mystery there, just math and honesty. No new PRs this week, but a 168 kg leg press and a 107.9 kg trap bar deadlift are still sitting there as the baseline, solid and real, proof of what the body already knows how to do. Four sessions next week — not because the numbers demand it, but because the guy who walks into that gym is getting more interesting every time he goes.
